lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: You really think someone's poisoning the well?

lines.speaker: elara
lines.text: I KNOW someone is. The question is who benefits from a dead village.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Everyone with a grudge and a bottle of nightcap, apparently.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Another sermon today. Third this week.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: My wife went. Says he's right — the crops dying IS punishment.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Punishment for what? Being poor?

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: The Miller family left yesterday. Just packed up and walked east.

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: Can't blame them. Three children and nothing to feed them.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: If the lord doesn't act soon, we'll all be walking.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Who would burn the granary? That was everything we had.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Someone who wants us desperate. And it's working.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Ironmarch riders on the east road again. Third time this week.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: They're counting our walls. Measuring us for a coffin.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: We don't even HAVE walls worth counting.

lines.speaker: old_maren
lines.text: The roots are closer today. Can you feel them?

lines.speaker: elara
lines.text: I can see them. They broke through the eastern fence overnight.

lines.speaker: old_maren
lines.text: The forest is tired of waiting. It's coming to collect.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Did you hear? The priest — he's Old Maren's grandson.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Silas's boy? The one the Church took?

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: The same. And Elara's his sister. She doesn't know yet.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: God help this village. We break everything we touch.

lines.speaker: fenrick
lines.text: The last three leave tomorrow. Before dawn.

lines.speaker: unknown
lines.text: The youngest won't stop crying for her mother.

lines.speaker: fenrick
lines.text: ...Tell her mother's waiting at the end of the road. It's kinder than the truth.
